data.title: Quanex updates 2025 guidance to $1.82B revenue and $235M adjusted EBITDA amid integration progress and operational challenges
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Quanex Building Products Corporation (NX) Q3 2025

Management View

  • George Wilson, President, CEO & Chairman, emphasized the company's resilience despite macro headwinds, noting "new operating segments are in place, synergy realization remains compelling and the cash flow generation of the combined entity has been strong." Wilson highlighted the integration of Tyman and the realization of cost synergies, stating, "After factoring in these additional synergies, mainly related to headcount, adjusting for lower volumes and pushing out the timing of when we should realize procurement savings, we still see a path to realizing approximately $45 million in cost synergies related to the Tyman acquisition over time."
  • Wilson detailed operational challenges at the window and door hardware business in Monterrey, Mexico, which "negatively impacted EBITDA in the Hardware Solutions segment by almost $5 million in the third quarter alone." He added, "We made leadership changes and are dedicating additional resources and capital to the facility to address and resolve these issues in an expedited manner."
  • Wilson reported a noncash goodwill impairment following the resegmentation of business units, clarifying "this impairment is not related to any performance indicators or changes to the long-term profitability expectations for our business."
  • CFO Scott Zuehlke stated, "On a consolidated basis, we reported net sales of $495.3 million during the third quarter of 2025, which represents an increase of approximately 77% compared to $280.3 million for the same period of 2024." Zuehlke attributed the increase mainly to the Tyman acquisition and discussed the $302.3 million noncash goodwill impairment, relating it to a lower market-based valuation due to share price at the trigger date.

Outlook

  • The company updated its fiscal 2025 guidance, now estimating "net sales of approximately $1.82 billion, which we expect will yield adjusted EBITDA of approximately $235 million" (Scott Zuehlke).
  • Zuehlke provided additional modeling assumptions for the full year 2025, including a gross margin of approximately 27%, SG&A of approximately $264 million, adjusted D&A of approximately $58 million, interest expense of approximately $53 million, an adjusted tax rate of 24.5%, CapEx of approximately $75 million and free cash flow of approximately $80 million.
  • Wilson indicated "volumes are expected to remain soft through the end of the year," but expressed confidence in a recovery when interest rates drop and consumer confidence improves.

Financial Results

  • Net sales for Q3 2025 were $495.3 million, with adjusted EBITDA at $70.3 million, up 67.2% year-over-year, both driven by the Tyman acquisition and cost synergies.
  • Adjusted net income for Q3 2025 was $31.6 million or $0.69 per diluted share, compared to $26.9 million or $0.81 per diluted share in Q3 2024.
  • Hardware Solutions segment generated $227.1 million in net sales, with adjusted EBITDA of $24.7 million, impacted by the Monterrey facility issues.
  • Extruded Solutions segment reported $174.4 million in revenue and adjusted EBITDA of $37.1 million.
  • Custom Solutions segment had $102.3 million in net sales and $12.9 million in adjusted EBITDA.
  • Free cash flow for Q3 2025 was $46.2 million, and $51.25 million of bank debt was repaid.
  • The company repurchased 100,000 shares for approximately $2.1 million, with $33.6 million remaining under the repurchase program.

Q&A

  • Steven Ramsey, Thompson Research Group, LLC, asked about competitive landscape changes and Mexico headwinds. George Wilson responded that "the softness that we see is more specifically related to the softness in both R&R and new construction," and confirmed no destocking was observed.
  • Ramsey further asked about Tyman Mexico's EBITDA headwind. Zuehlke noted, "We do expect an impact in the fourth quarter. It may be similar to 3Q, depending on the progress that we show during the quarter."
  • Reuben Garner, The Benchmark Company, LLC, inquired about the balance of lower-than-expected results. Zuehlke cited "split between market and then procurement synergies specifically" as key factors.
  • Adam Thalhimer, Thompson, Davis & Company, Inc., asked about Q4 top line drivers and cash flow priorities. Zuehlke replied, "continuing to strengthen our balance sheet in this environment is a top priority."
  • Julio Romero, Sidoti & Company, LLC, sought details on Monterrey operational issues and Tyman integration. Wilson described the tooling and equipment problems and emphasized the mix of manual assembly and injection molding at Monterrey, with investments underway to resolve issues.
  • Romero also asked about the timeline for the first tranche of synergies. Zuehlke replied, "early 2026. I think that's still pretty accurate."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst questions focused on demand weakness, operational issues in Mexico, and guidance adjustments, displaying a slightly negative to neutral tone as they pressed for clarity on remediation and recovery timelines.
  • Management maintained a confident stance during prepared remarks, repeatedly emphasizing operational progress, cost synergy realization, and a strong balance sheet. In the Q&A, management's tone was more cautious, acknowledging challenges while reiterating focus on recovery and customer service, especially regarding Monterrey.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from highly optimistic about integration progress and synergy capture to a more defensive posture as they addressed operational setbacks and revised guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw a downward revision in full-year guidance to $1.82 billion in net sales and $235 million in adjusted EBITDA, compared to the prior range of $1.84–$1.86 billion and $270–$280 million, reflecting softer demand and operational challenges.
  • The strategic focus has shifted toward resolving the Monterrey facility's issues and maintaining cost synergy realization, compared to the prior quarter's emphasis on integration progress and margin expansion.
  • Analysts in the current quarter focused more heavily on the impact and resolution timeline of operational disruptions, while previous quarter questions centered on integration synergies and tariff mitigation opportunities.
  • Management's overall confidence remains, but the language around short-term headwinds and recovery timelines is more measured than in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management explicitly pointed to macroeconomic headwinds, including soft consumer confidence, delayed construction activity, and tariff uncertainties in both North America and Europe.
  • The Monterrey, Mexico, facility's tooling and equipment issues have created inefficiencies, higher costs, and a significant EBITDA impact, with continued pressure expected in Q4.
  • Management has made leadership changes and committed additional resources and capital to address the Monterrey challenges, with expectations for tangible benefits in early fiscal 2026.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the pace of recovery, synergy realization, and the potential impact of industry consolidation among OEM customers.

Final Takeaway

Quanex’s third quarter was defined by successful integration progress, strong synergy capture, and robust cash flow, but was also tempered by operational setbacks in the Monterrey hardware business and persistent macroeconomic headwinds. Management emphasized confidence in the company’s long-term positioning, a clear path to $45 million in cost synergies, and the expectation that operational improvements and demand recovery will drive growth as the macro environment stabilizes.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: FedEx heads into fiscal Q1 results with the bar lowered
data.description:
With the company set to report fiscal first quarter earnings on September 18, FedEx (NYSE:FDX) faces many hurdles to an upside surprise, namely stagnant business-to-business demand, deteriorating business-to-consumer (B2C) trends, as well as pressures from U.S. trade policies that have stifled shipping from overseas customers.

“The bar has been lowered,” J.P. Morgan’s Brian Ossenbeck says, as sentiment has remained “broadly negative on the sub-sector” and management’s mid-August comments on the softening B2C demand lowered the FQ1 bar even further.

After the company reported fiscal fourth quarter results, shares retreated by as much as 6% despite a beat on both EPS and revenue as the company’s sales and profit guidance came in less than Wall Street expected. And with sentiment largely negative for parcel stocks, Ossenbeck expects a miss on FQ1.

“Our FY26EPS forecast moves lower to $17.50 on our reduced FQ1 estimates which we believe will set the stage for a lower base for the remainder of the year with no clear catalyst for industrial demand to meaningfully improve,” he writes.

Despite the gloomy outlook for FQ1, analysts remain bullish on FedEx’s (NYSE:FDX) future, including Ossenbeck who maintains his Overweight rating as the stock “looks attractive on a discounted [sum of the parts] basis with the Freight spin and the Network 2.0 integration.”

The spin-off of the freight segment is a “key catalyst” to potentially unlock significant shareholder value as well as raise FedEx’s (NYSE:FDX) valuation multiple, says Seeking Alpha analyst TradingKey, while Seeking Alpha analyst Ray Merola weighs in on DRIVE; “FedEx’s DRIVE initiative is delivering on cost reductions…these savings are demonstrable, not hot air.”

While competition is still a hurdle, along with the tariff outlook and business backdrop – all of which management will likely address on the earnings call – the company still maintains an advantage in its Express network which accounts for more than half its business.

And by 2027, the DRIVE and Network 2.0 initiatives will deliver ~$6B in savings, which will compensate for tariff headwinds and the USPS loss, presenting a “balanced but cautious investment case,” says Seeking Alpha analyst Market Mind.

So, despite the negative view into FQ1 results, J.P. Morgan’s Ossenbeck sees upside in the stock’s valuation warranted by the Freight spin-off and long-term potential in combining the Ground and Express networks.

FedEx (FDX) is expected to report an adjusted profit of $3.67 in the fiscal first quarter on $21.71B in revenue, an improvement of 2% and 0.5%, respectively, from the same quarter last year.

FedEx (FDX) shares are down 19% YTD versus -32.3% for competitor UPS and a 10% gain for the S&P 500.

data.title: FDA targets illegal imported GLP-1 ingredients
data.description:
The U.S. FDA has begun a crackdown on illegally imported active pharmaceutical ingredients used to make GLP-1 weight loss drugs.

Although Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) market their own GLP-1 obesity therapies, Wegovy (semaglutide) and Zepbound (tirzepatide), the two companies have been contending with copycat versions sold on the Internet that are compounded.

The FDA is creating a "green list" of GLP-1 APIs from foreign facilities that the agency has either inspected or has determined are in compliance with its standards.

The agency added that compounded versions of Novo and Lilly's therapies should only be used when an individual's medical needs cannot be supported by the name-brand drugs.

The FDA also noted it is aware of issues with compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide such as dosing errors and adverse events, including some requiring hospitalization.

Telehealth outlets such as as Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) were making compounded versions of GLP-1 drugs while both were in shortage, which is legal. Despite legal challenges to allow compounding pharmacies to continue doing so after the ending of the shortage, courts have ruled against the pharmacies.

data.title: NiSource downgraded at Jefferies on rising Indiana regulatory, political risk
data.description:
NiSource (NYSE:NI) shares are downgraded to Hold from Buy with a $44 price target, lowered from $48, at Jefferies, a day after falling 5% following news that two of the five Indiana utility commissioners and the rate pay advocate are stepping down in moves initiated by Governor Braun, reflecting uncertainty amid the increasingly political nature of Indiana’s regulatory landscape.

Investors feared the regulatory changes potentially could delay NiSource's (NYSE:NI) application for the Genco spinoff, which is seen as a key catalyst for the company's stock performance.

Commissioners Freeman and Bennett announced they will step down in October 2025, well ahead of their 2026 term expiration, and Abby Gray was appointed to lead the OUCC with a mandate to cut bills, moves that Jefferies analyst Julien Dumoulin-Smith said in his downgrade are highly unusual and increase uncertainty.

Even after Thursday's sharp selloff, the stock still trades at one of the highest relative premiums across the utilities sector, and Dumoulin-Smith said he sees a "muted chance for shares to regain their historic premium anew given the meaningful uncertainty."

data.title: ASML rises as UBS upgrades on belief risks are 'well understood'
data.description:
ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) shares were in focus on Friday as UBS upgraded the Dutch semiconductor equipment firm on the belief that risks associated with the decline in lithography intensity and concerns about China are “well understood” by the market.

Shares rose 3% in midday trading.

“Given ASML's long product lead times and high level of integration into customers' long-term roadmaps, the market is likely to look through a relatively well telegraphed weak [2026E] to [2027E], when we see the return of ASML as a quality compounder delivering 20% EPS CAGR 26-30E,” UBS analysts wrote in a note to clients.

The analysts upgraded ASML — which makes extreme lithography machines used by foundries such as Intel (INTC) and Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) — to Buy from Neutral and upped the price target to €750 from €660.

Delving a little deeper, the analysts said that litho intensity is likely to start from 2027 on, with the key drive being the production ramp of the A14 node at Taiwan Semiconductor. As such, there could be an uptick in extreme lithography exposure for the A14 node. There's also a relief that the spending uncertainty surrounding Intel and Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) appear to be “fading,” either with the two companies coming back or Taiwan Semiconductor taking more share.

Other positives that ASML has going for it include further confidence on High NA, with an adoption of small number of layers going into production into 2028 and 2029.

“Our analysis of Low vs. High NA production ramp timelines and Intel's progress as a first mover suggests meaningful adoption in the next two years is highly likely,” the analysts explained. “We forecast 6/10 High NA shipments in 2027/28, representing [circa] 30% of group revenue growth in 2027-28E or 1-3 percentage points of share potential gain of [wafer fab equipment].”

Other catalysts include upcoming quarterly earnings results; commentary on high NA adoption; the launch of the new higher-priced low NA-EUV-F model in 2026; and new customer announcements from Intel and Samsung through next year.

data.title: Market Voices: Job number revisions, Hyundai ICE raid, DOD name change
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of announcements, statements and remarks that could impact markets, sectors or individual stocks. 

  • White House economic advisor Kevin Hassett said that he expects Friday's disappointing job report numbers to be revised upwards.

Earlier Friday, the Bureau of Labor Statistics reported that non-farm payrolls increased by 22,000 in August. Analysts had been expecting growth of around 75,000.

"It's a little bit of a disappointing job number, but I pretty much expect it's going to revise up," Hassett told CNBC during an interview. 

  • South Korea confirmed South Korean nationals were detained during an immigration raid on the construction site of a Hyundai-LG Energy battery plant in Georgia.

U.S. immigration officials arrested around 475 workers at the site, including over 300 South Korean nationals, according to The Wall Street Journal.  

"The economic activities of our companies investing in the U.S. and the rights and interests of our nationals must not be unfairly violated," South Korean Foreign Affairs Ministry spokesperson Lee Jae-woong said during a press briefing, according to South Korea's Yonhap News Agency. 

"We conveyed our concern and regret through the U.S. Embassy in Seoul today," Lee added.

Yonhap also said South Korean officials are setting up an on-site task force to address the situation. 

South Korea agreed to invest $350 billion in the U.S. as part of its recently negotiated trade deal. The U.S. tariff on South Korean imports was set at 15%.

  • President Trump intends to sign an executive order on Friday that allows the Department of Defense to be referred to as the Department of War, a name not used for the department since 1949.

The executive order will authorize "the Secretary of Defense, the Department of Defense, and subordinate officials to use secondary titles such as ‘Secretary of War,’ ‘Department of War,’ and ‘Deputy Secretary of War’ in official correspondence, public communications, ceremonial contexts, and non-statutory documents within the executive branch,” according to Barron's.

“The United States military is the strongest and most lethal fighting force in the world [and] should have a name that reflects its unmatched power and readiness," the order adds, according to Barron's.

Barron's noted that officially changing the name of the Department of Defense would require an act of Congress. The use of secondary titles, however, does not require congressional approval.

data.title: Argan stock drops as revenue misses estimates; backlog and project timing in focus
data.description:
Argan (NYSE:AGX) shares declined in Friday morning trading after the company reported quarterly revenue below analyst expectations, despite posting a profit beat. Investors are now focused on the sustainability of its backlog, profit margins, and the timing of its project pipeline.

Argan (NYSE:AGX), up 52% year-to-date, dropped 13% on Friday. Despite the pullback, both Seeking Alpha and Wall Street analysts maintain a Buy rating on the stock.

The company outlined a record $2B backlog and targets further growth through natural gas projects; however, management acknowledged the "lumpy nature of this business, especially this construction business," and the challenges in providing precise margin or revenue guidance.

There was also recognition of risks tied to project timing, execution, and market demand, but management emphasized their "disciplined approach when pursuing any project" and their focus on "minimizing costly project overruns."

Analysts raised questions about margin sustainability and the cadence of backlog additions, reflecting concern over potential volatility in financial performance.

The company aims to maintain its presence in the renewable energy sector and expects natural gas projects to be the main driver of growth in the foreseeable future, CEO David Watson said on the conference call.

data.title: Alibaba in focus as Qwen unveils preview of new AI model with 1T parameters
data.description:
• Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) was in focus on Friday as the Chinese tech company's Qwen unit shared a preview of its newest model, Qwen3-Max-Preview.
  • Shares rose 3.3% in midday trading. 
  • The model, which was announced on the social network X, has more than 1T parameters, Alibaba said. “Benchmarks show it beats our previous best, Qwen3-235B-A22B-2507," the company explained in the post. "Internal tests + early user feedback confirm: stronger performance, broader knowledge, better at conversations, agentic tasks & instruction following. “Scaling works — and the official release will surprise you even more. Stay tuned!”
  • Alibaba also compared the new model to that of competitors, such as DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK), Anthropic, and Kimi K2.
  • The company did not say when the new model would be publicly available. Alibaba has not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: AMD, Nvidia slump as Broadcom gets new customer for AI chip
data.description:
Shares of Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) and Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) fell after a report that OpenAI is set to produce its first AI chip next year in collaboration with Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO).

AMD slumped about 5.5%, while Nvidia's shares fell nearly 3%. Meanwhile, Broadcom's stock jumped nearly 10% on Friday. 

Broadcom, which reported its third quarter results on Thursday, said it had signed an unnamed fourth customer for its application-specific integrated circuits, or XPUs. 

“Now further to these 3 customers, as we had previously mentioned, we have been working with other prospects on their own AI accelerators,” Broadcom's President and CEO Hock Tan said on the company's earnings call. “Last quarter, one of these prospects released production orders to Broadcom, and we have accordingly characterized them as a qualified customer for XPUs and, in fact, have secured over $10 billion of orders of AI racks based on our XPUs.” 

“And reflecting this, we now expect the outlook for our fiscal 2026 AI revenue to improve significantly from what we had indicated last quarter,” Tan added.

Wall Street analysts were bullish following Broadcom's surprise announcement. 

“AVGO delivered another solid beat-and-raise w/[with] continued custom AI XPU momentum — most notably highlighting $10B+ order from new (4th) customer for inference-focused XPU; accelerating AI growth in FY26 and again in FY27,” said Wells analysts led by Aaron Rakers.

Wells Fargo's analysts suggested the OpenAI chip would be inference-focused, but Broadcom did not explicitly mention this. 

Seeking Alpha has put in a request for comment to Broadcom about the identity of the customer and that they would be used for inferencing. 

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Joseph Moore, said the “headline grabber will be the addition of a 4th customer for AI ASIC processors, reported in the press as Open AI,” driving a surprising $10B of second half of 2026 revenue.

Tan noted on the earnings call that the company's XPU business accelerated to 65% of its AI revenue in the third quarter. “Demand for custom AI accelerators from our 3 customers continue to grow as each of them journeys at their own pace towards compute self-sufficiency,” Tan added.

Both Nvidia and AMD provide AI chips to tech companies — including hyperscalers — which use them for both training AI models and inferencing. Nvidia's latest AI chip technology is based on the Blackwell platform, while AMD's latest AI chips are the Instinct MI350 series.

data.title: Legend Biotech considering second stock listing outside US
data.description:
• Legend Biotech (NASDAQ:LEGN) is said to be weighing a secondary listing of its stock in a market outside of the US.
  • Hong Kong, London, and Singapore exchanges are being considered, according to a report in Bloomberg.
  • Although the company has had talks with advisors, it may eventually decide not to proceed with another listing, sources told the news service. 
  • Legend Biotech helped develop the CAR-T therapy Carvykti (ciltacabtagene autoleucel), which is co-marketed with its partner, Johnson & Johnson (JNJ). It is approved as a second-line therapy for relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma.
  • In Q2, Legend reported $439M in net trade sales of Carvykti.
